name: Khaṇḍadeva
dates_notes: Conventionally dated c. 1600–1665 CE on the basis of his association with the Vārāṇasī Mīmāṃsā circle of the early seventeenth century and on the colophon evidence in the *Bhāṭṭa-Dīpikā*. The standard Mīmāṃsā secondary literature accepts this window (Verpoorten 1987, Mīmāṃsā Literature in the History of Indian Literature series).
core_thesis: Khaṇḍadeva is the principal early-modern Bhāṭṭa Mīmāṃsā commentator and the author of the most influential post-Pārthasārathi Mīmāṃsā treatises. The *Bhāṭṭa-Dīpikā* (Lamp of the Bhāṭṭa Position) is a systematic commentary on the *Jaimini-Sūtra* organized adhikaraṇa by adhikaraṇa, articulating the Bhāṭṭa position with the technical precision of seventeenth-century Vārāṇasī scholasticism — the same intellectual milieu that produced the mature Navya-Nyāya of Gadādhara and Raghunātha and the Advaita engagement with Navya-Nyāya methods of Dharmarāja Adhvarīndra. The *Bhāṭṭa-Kaustubha* (Jewel of the Bhāṭṭa Position) is the parallel topical treatise on the disputed Bhāṭṭa–Prābhākara questions. Khaṇḍadeva's distinctive contribution to mature Mīmāṃsā is the use of Navya-Nyāya logical-analytic methods to articulate Mīmāṃsā doctrines with a precision unavailable to the medieval tradition — the *abhihitānvaya* theory of meaning is restated with the Navya-Nyāya apparatus of *paryāptyananta-vyāpyatva* and *avacchedaka-bheda*; the *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* doctrine is restated with explicit attention to the alternative *paratah* analyses available in the contemporary Navya-Nyāya literature. The treatises are the standard mature Bhāṭṭa references for the early-modern period and the principal source through which the school enters the modern scholastic tradition.

engaged_works.title: Bhatta-Dipika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored by Khaṇḍadeva. Sanskrit text extant in multiple editions (Chowkhamba Sanskrit Series; the Madras Government Oriental Series).

ascription_evidence.description: Verpoorten 1987 reference; standard early-modern Mīmāṃsā doxography.

engaged_works.summary: A systematic adhikaraṇa-by-adhikaraṇa commentary on the *Jaimini-Sūtra* articulating the Bhāṭṭa position with seventeenth-century Vārāṇasī scholastic precision. The treatise's distinctive feature is its use of Navya-Nyāya logical-analytic methods to clarify the Mīmāṃsā doctrines — definitions are stated with explicit attention to *lakṣaṇa-doṣas* (faults of definition), arguments are restated with the Navya-Nyāya apparatus of pervasion and conditioning relations, and the principal Bhāṭṭa positions are defended against both the Prābhākara alternatives and the contemporary Navya-Nyāya critiques. The treatment of the first adhyāya is the most philosophically dense; the remaining adhyāyas treat the standard Mīmāṃsā ritual-categories with the same scholastic precision.

engaged_works.title: Bhatta-Kaustubha
engaged_works.summary: A topical treatise on the disputed Bhāṭṭa–Prābhākara questions. The treatise covers the principal disputed points — the *abhihitānvaya*/*anvitābhidhāna* analysis of *śabda-bodha*, the *anyathā-khyāti*/*akhyāti* analysis of error, the *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* analysis of valid cognition — with the Bhāṭṭa position defended in each case. The treatise is shorter and more polemically focused than the *Bhāṭṭa-Dīpikā* and is the natural starting point for the study of Khaṇḍadeva's position.

reception_notes: Khaṇḍadeva is the standard early-modern Bhāṭṭa author and is cited extensively in the contemporary Vārāṇasī scholastic literature. His engagement with Navya-Nyāya methods is parallel to Āpadeva's *Mīmāṃsā-Nyāya-Prakāśa* — Āpadeva supplies the more accessible introduction, Khaṇḍadeva the more technically demanding treatment. The two together are the principal early-modern Bhāṭṭa textbooks and the standard references for the modern study of the school.
